The whole decoration of a temple consisted therefore in an act of adoration by the monarch repeated in various forms. The piety and gratitude of the monarch were reflected in the grandeur of the great festivals, which took place at the temple on multiple occasions throughout the year. The ceremonies primarily consisted of grand processions that emerged from the sanctuary, were arranged in the hypostyle hall, and then proceeded through the open courtyards situated between the temple's structures and the enclosing wall. The encircling bands of porticos seemed to invite all who approached to seek shelter beneath their galleries. The play of light and shadow created by the alternate voids and solids within them charmed all who beheld them.
